article.title: Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API

harvestedTerms.summary: OpenAI announces three new realtime audio API models: GPT-Realtime-2 (voice reasoning/agentic), GPT-Realtime-Translate (live speech translation across 70+ languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (streaming transcription). The article introduces voice-to-action, systems-to-voice, and voice-to-voice as emerging developer patterns, and highlights agentic voice features like parallel tool calls, preambles, and adjustable reasoning effort.

article.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X

harvestedTerms.summary: A hackathon project write-up describing MachinaCheck, a multi-agent CNC manufacturability analysis system built on AMD MI300X hardware using Qwen 2.5 7B, LangChain, and vLLM. The system parses STEP CAD files and runs a five-stage pipeline mixing deterministic Python logic with LLM-based reasoning agents. The article is primarily a technical how-to/demo piece rather than a conceptual contribution.

article.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage

harvestedTerms.summary: Podcast episode covering GPT-5.5 release with system card and chain-of-thought monitorability discussion, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast launch, DeepSeek V4 open-sourcing with MoE and 1M-token context, a new long-horizon agent benchmark (Clawmark), and policy/safety research on AI sabotage, document delegation corruption, and bit-flip attacks on neural networks.

A Stratechery weekly digest covering Big Tech Q1 earnings, AI infrastructure spending (CapEx comparisons), Microsoft's agentic business model framing, Amazon's inference-era positioning, and an interview with Joanna Stern about her AI book. The article is a content roundup with brief summaries of underlying pieces rather than substantive analysis itself.

article.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.

harvestedTerms.summary: Google blog post announcing 'The Small Brief,' an initiative pairing three advertising industry veterans with small businesses to create AI-assisted ad campaigns using Google Flow. The post is essentially a product initiative announcement with minimal substantive AI content.

A short quotation post from Simon Willison's blog, citing Andrew Quinn on the philosophy of learning by doing — specifically the 'wheel reinvention' heuristic for reaching genuine expertise. No AI-specific technical content; the excerpt touches on programming epistemology and learning strategies. No new or longlist terms materially used.
